_README: Default PenDonn config. Empty MACs let interface_manager fall back to interface NAMES (less reliable across USB reseats — set monitor_mac/attack_mac/management_mac to pin by MAC). Empty secret_key triggers a per-startup random secret (sessions reset on restart) — set a persistent value in config.json.local. See config/config.example.json for an annotated reference.

wifi._comment: Interface names can swap on reboot — populate monitor_mac/attack_mac/management_mac for stable detection. management_interface MUST point to whichever iface carries SSH; safety preflight will refuse to start otherwise.

allowlist._comment: SSIDs that the daemon is ALLOWED to target (scan, deauth, capture, crack, enumerate). When `strict` is true (default), the daemon ONLY touches networks in this list — empty list = scan-only mode, nothing gets attacked. When `strict` is false, the list is ignored and EVERY visible SSID is fair game (requires safety.armed_override; refuses to start otherwise). The legacy `whitelist` key is still accepted as an alias for back-compat.

cracking._engines_comment: Tried in order. cowpatty: reads .pcapng natively, pure CPU, reliable on Raspberry Pi 4. aircrack-ng: .cap fallback. john: last resort. hashcat removed — local hashcat + PoCL segfaults on ARM64; will be re-added as remote-hashcat feature.

web._comment: host defaults to 127.0.0.1 (loopback only). Wizard sets 0.0.0.0 for LAN access. Empty secret_key triggers ephemeral per-startup secret. Set password via scripts/hash-password.py and store in config.json.local.

basic_auth._comment: Set enabled=true and fill password_hash in config.json.local (run scripts/hash-password.py). WARNING: host=0.0.0.0 without auth exposes the UI to the whole LAN.

safety._comment: SSH-lockout protection. Defaults are intentionally strict. See docs/SAFETY.md before changing anything here.
